post_text:
📌 RAG Systems

We're at the peak of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) hype 🚀, but most teams are still struggling to move beyond fine-tuning 🤔.

I've seen companies waste months on fine-tuning, only to realize that RAG is the better approach 📊.

The key to building production RAG systems lies in vector databases, chunking strategies, re-ranking, and evaluation metrics 📈.

A basic RAG setup involves storing documents in a vector database, which is then queried by a large language model (LLM) 📄.

However, this naive approach can be improved by introducing a reranker that filters retrieved context, ensuring that only relevant information is considered 📝.

Multimodal RAG takes this a step further by incorporating text, images, audio, and video into the retrieval process 📹.

RAG Architecture:
Documents
Vector DB
LLM
Reranker
Multimodal Input
Graph RAG and hybrid RAG offer even more advanced capabilities, leveraging knowledge graphs and structured retrieval to improve performance 📊.

Agentic RAG and multi-agent RAG introduce agent-based routing and collaboration, allowing for more complex and dynamic retrieval decisions 🤖.

I've seen teams achieve significant improvements in retrieval accuracy by implementing these strategies 📈.

So, what's your approach to building production RAG systems?

💬 What's your vector database of choice — Faiss, Pinecone, or something custom? 📁

#LLM #GenAI #VectorDB #AIEngineering #RAG
